name: Nested Encryption Workflow
description:
Set up defense-in-depth encryption by creating a VeraCrypt volume nested inside another VeraCrypt volume.

Scenario: A security researcher needs to store classified source documents behind two independent encryption layers with different passwords. The outer volume already exists; you must create an inner volume inside it, populate it with sensitive files, and cleanly tear down both layers.

Starting State:
- VeraCrypt GUI is running
- Outer volume exists at /home/ga/Volumes/outer_vault.hc (50MB, password: OuterVault2024!)
- Sensitive files at /home/ga/Documents/sensitive_files/:
  - SF312_Nondisclosure_Agreement.txt
  - FY2024_Revenue_Budget.csv
- Mount points available: /home/ga/MountPoints/slot1/ and /home/ga/MountPoints/slot2/

Steps:
1. Mount the outer volume /home/ga/Volumes/outer_vault.hc to /home/ga/MountPoints/slot1/ using password: OuterVault2024!
2. Create a new 20MB encrypted volume at /home/ga/MountPoints/slot1/inner_vault.hc with:
   - Encryption: Serpent
   - Hash: SHA-512
   - Filesystem: FAT
   - Password: InnerSecret!99
   - PIM: 0 (default), no keyfiles
3. Mount the inner volume /home/ga/MountPoints/slot1/inner_vault.hc to /home/ga/MountPoints/slot2/ using password: InnerSecret!99
4. Copy both files from /home/ga/Documents/sensitive_files/ into /home/ga/MountPoints/slot2/
5. Dismount the inner volume (slot2) first, then dismount the outer volume (slot1)

Final State: No VeraCrypt volumes mounted. The outer vault contains inner_vault.hc which contains both sensitive documents.
